evals.prompt: We've never done formal customer research. I want to understand why customers choose us over competitors and what almost made them not buy. How do I set up a win-loss interview program?
evals.expected_output: Win-loss interview program design with participant criteria, interview questions, cadence, analysis framework, and how to use insights across marketing and product.
assertions: Provides specific interview question list for win and loss interviews
Recommends interview cadence (how many per month)
Provides analysis and insight synthesis framework
Notes how to distribute insights to marketing, sales, and product

evals.prompt: I want to run a customer survey to understand satisfaction. Should I use NPS or CSAT?
evals.expected_output: Comparison of NPS vs. CSAT with guidance on when to use each, how to implement, and what to do with the results beyond the score.
assertions: Explains NPS (loyalty/recommendation metric) vs. CSAT (satisfaction metric)
Gives clear guidance on when each is appropriate
Notes that the follow-up question is more valuable than the score
Addresses survey frequency and response rate optimization

evals.prompt: Create a customer persona for our product
evals.expected_output: Notes that customer research informs personas but persona creation itself is a marketing strategy task. Offers to design a research program to gather data for an evidence-based persona rather than a fictional one.
assertions: Distinguishes between research-based and assumption-based personas
Offers to design a research program to build an evidence-based persona
Does not just generate a fictional persona template
